speaker: Christina
text: I-I've got to clean up in here.

speaker: Narrator
text:
One look at the assorted piles of rubbish dissuades Christina from that idea. Instead, she bargains that a focused attack on her project will relieve most of the stress weighing down on her. 
	
	She turns on the livestream.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Pasaya's just started, and as always, she's pontificating on the nature of things in our reality. 
	
	This was her way of allowing fans to prepare themselves in lobbies ahead of time for her multiplayer sessions. Either that or she's sorting out some technical difficulties. 
	
	Either way, Christina didn't have it in her to waste an evening on a vain chance to game with Pasaya. Even if she really, really wanted to.

speaker: Pasaya
text: I'm not worried about the claim, ya'll save these shits anyway, just upload it on one of those channels.

speaker: Pasaya
text: This is precisely the point where they ran out of manga-

speaker: Pasaya
text: I know the dude was a freak, I'm not saying you should give that guy any of your money.

speaker: Narrator
text:
She thumbs through more of Blanche's notes. Copying some of the most useful parts of the data onto her own documents. 
	
	Through this, she uncovered more interesting details about the parasite afflicting the fish.

speaker: Pasaya
text: I'm apologizing in advance, my chat-drones.

speaker: Pasaya
text: For the first few hours today I am locking in on a...

speaker: Pasaya
text: If you want me to beat you in any way, you're gonna hafta get ready now..

speaker: Narrator
text:
Blanche reiterates at several points that the creature's nervous system is sophisticated in a way that is comparable to the most intelligent cephalopods.
	
	It further muddies the waters of what exactly this thing is, she notes that the creature's main form of survival involves attaching itself to the brain.

speaker: Narrator
text:
"It dangles down the length of the fish and uses tendrils extended from its body to intertwine itself with the organs of the host creature.
	
	At the end of its body is what seems to be a second mouth capable of taking nutrients from or sharing with the host body. 
	
	It's capable of expanding and contracting itself to allow for easier digestion of the host's food."

speaker: Narrator
text:
"After examining one particular fish it had proboscises rooted into its flesh reminiscent of that of the ribbon worm  
	
	There were capillary-like wounds left behind after its tendrils were pulled out of the fish's dorsal. 
	
	It seemed that they had grown out and mainly wrapped around key parts of its nervous system such as the vagus nerve and the brain's hypothalamus."

speaker: Narrator
text: Like a switch being flipped in her mind, Christina jolted up after a sharp pain in her stomach. It pulled her down out of her grease-stained chair, writhing on the floor in pain.
